name: Swift Excellence
description: Ten-gate Swift enforcement: SPM architecture, performance, portability, safety, testing, CI, DocC documentation, API design, release readiness, truth. Zero Xcode dependencies, zero completion handlers, zero retain cycles, low memory, low CPU.

phases.name: Architecture & Idioms
phases.principle: Is the structure sound, modular, and idiomatic?

delegates.task_template: Enforce strict Swift Package Manager workflow for: ${goal}. Remove any .xcodeproj, .xcworkspace, .pbxproj files. MUST build via swift build and swift test only. Define modular targets in Package.swift (reference: AudioWaveLib pattern with Core, IO, Utilities, Extensions). Each module has single responsibility and minimal public API. Dependency graph depth <=4. swift-tools-version 5.9+. Flag modules >500 lines, circular dependencies, targets importing >3 internal modules. Products MUST specify .static or .dynamic. Executables use @main. Migrate ALL completion handlers to async/await. Enforce Actor isolation for mutable shared state. Verify Sendable conformance on concurrency boundaries. -strict-concurrency=complete MUST pass. Score Tech Debt (target 10/10) and Modularity (target 100/100).
verifies: Actor isolation for shared mutable state

delegates.task_template: Enforce Swift idioms across the entire codebase for: ${goal}. Flag: class where struct suffices (no identity, no inheritance), force-unwrap (!) without justification, force-cast (as!) without justification, force-try (try!) without justification, completion handlers instead of async/await, manual reference counting, mutable state where immutable suffices, stringly-typed APIs instead of enums. Enforce: guard-let for early exit, defer for cleanup, protocol-oriented design over class inheritance, value types by default, Codable for serialization, Result for error propagation. Verify Swift naming: camelCase for functions/variables, PascalCase for types, omit needless words.
verifies: structs preferred over classes where possible

delegates.task_template: Conduct deep research on current Swift best practices for: ${goal}. Research: latest Swift concurrency patterns (actors, structured concurrency, isolation regions), SPM plugin ecosystem, Swift-DocC CLI capabilities, cross-platform Linux support status and Foundation feature parity, current SwiftLint vs swift-format comparison, latest Swift API Design Guidelines updates, Swift Evolution proposals for upcoming features. Cross-validate findings against Swift.org documentation, WWDC sessions, and Swift Evolution proposals. Flag deprecated patterns and propose modern replacements.

Organize Sources/ and Tests/ for strict modularity for: ${goal}. Mirror AudioWaveLib pattern: every Sources/<ModuleName>/ has corresponding Tests/<ModuleName>Tests/ in Package.swift. No source files at Sources/ root, no test files at Tests/ root. Test targets depend ONLY on their module plus shared test utilities. Directory names match Package.swift targets exactly (case-sensitive). Remove orphaned files not referenced by any target. Consolidate duplicated test helpers into SharedTestUtilities module.
verifies: every source module has test target
no files at Sources/ or Tests/ root
test targets isolated to their module

phases.checkpoint: SPM-pure, modular, async/await migrated, best practices researched, files organized
phases.failure_message: Architecture gate failed. Remove Xcode files, fix modularity, and migrate to async/await.

phases.name: Performance & Resources
phases.principle: Is every operation fast, lean, and resource-efficient?

delegates.task_template: Analyze Swift value types and memory for: ${goal}. Replace every class with struct where no identity/inheritance needed. Implement copy-on-write for large value types (isKnownUniquelyReferenced). Profile retain cycles: flag delegates not marked weak, closures without [weak self]. Verify: no retain cycles in Combine/async pipelines, autoreleasepool for batch processing, lazy properties for expensive computations, no unbounded collection growth. Run address sanitizer analysis. Benchmark hot paths with swift-benchmark or XCTest.measure. Profile build time: flag if debug >45s or release >180s. Target: low memory, low CPU, zero retain cycles.
verifies: classes replaced with structs where possible
no retain cycles (delegates weak, closures [weak self])

delegates.task_template: Audit Swift dependency health for: ${goal}. Review every dependency in Package.swift: verify .upToNextMajor or exact version (not .branch or .revision for production). Replace heavy frameworks with lighter alternatives or stdlib/Foundation equivalents. Flag total deps >15. Verify Package.resolved committed. Check all deps maintained (commits within 12 months). Verify release build uses -O or -Osize. Strip debug symbols in release. Verify no debug-only code leaks via #if DEBUG. Check binary size.
verifies: heavy deps replaced with stdlib alternatives

phases.checkpoint: Value types optimized, zero retain cycles, dependencies lean, builds fast
phases.failure_message: Performance gate failed. Retain cycles, slow builds, or dependency bloat detected.

phases.name: Portability & Platform Parity
phases.principle: Does it build and pass identically on every platform?

delegates.task_template: Verify cross-platform portability for: ${goal}. Ensure ALL imports are platform-agnostic in Core modules: use Foundation and Dispatch only, NEVER import Combine, SwiftUI, AppKit, UIKit, or Darwin-only frameworks. Platform-specific code MUST be isolated in dedicated targets gated with #if canImport(). Verify: Date/Calendar use Foundation, file ops use FileManager, networking uses URLSession or cross-platform library. Test on Linux (Docker swift:latest): no linker errors, no missing symbols, identical test results. Flag any #if os(macOS) in non-platform targets. Verify Package.swift avoids unnecessary .when(platforms:). No hardcoded paths. No Xcode-specific tooling required.

phases.checkpoint: Cross-platform parity verified on macOS and Linux
phases.failure_message: Portability gate failed. Linux build failures or platform-specific leaks detected.

phases.name: Safety & Security
phases.principle: Is the code safe and the supply chain clean?

delegates.task_template: Security audit of Swift codebase for: ${goal}. Audit every force-unwrap (!), force-cast (as!), force-try (try!): each MUST have documented justification or be replaced with safe alternatives (guard let, as?, do-catch). Scan for: unchecked array indexing, unvalidated URL construction, insecure network config (ATS exceptions, HTTP), hardcoded secrets, file operations without error handling, SQL injection in Core Data predicates. Verify: user input validated at boundaries, Codable handles malformed input. Run swift build -sanitize=address and -sanitize=thread.

Audit Swift supply chain security and define security policy for: ${goal}. Review every Package.swift dependency: verify .upToNextMajor or exact version, check for known vulnerabilities, verify maintained (commits within 12 months), check license compatibility. Flag deps with excessive transitive chains, unsafe Swift or C interop without justification, non-GitHub sources without verification. Verify Package.resolved committed. Audit any binaryTarget for provenance. Classify threat level using S0-S3 severity. Minimize count: flag >15, propose stdlib/Foundation alternatives.

phases.checkpoint: Zero unjustified force operations, supply chain clean, sanitizers pass
phases.failure_message: Safety gate failed. Unjustified force operations or dependency risks detected.

phases.name: Testing & Coverage
phases.principle: Is every code path tested with evidence?

delegates.task_template: Enforce comprehensive XCTest coverage for: ${goal}. Decouple logic tests from UI/AppKit/UIKit. Tests MUST run via swift test on Linux. Achieve 100% line coverage and 90%+ branch coverage (swift test --enable-code-coverage + llvm-cov). Generate property-based tests for: Codable roundtrips, Equatable/Hashable contracts, collection invariants. Test async: Task cancellation, actor isolation, structured concurrency cleanup. Parametrize edge cases: empty collections, nil optionals, Unicode, Int.max/Int.min, concurrent access. Enforce: no test interdependency, XCTAssertEqual over XCTAssert, every throws function tested. Run mutation testing to verify test effectiveness.
verifies: tests run via swift test on Linux

phases.checkpoint: 100% coverage, property tests, Linux parity, mutation tested
phases.failure_message: Testing gate failed. Coverage gaps or missing test categories detected.

phases.name: CI & Automation
phases.principle: Does CI enforce every standard automatically?

delegates.task_template: Enforce Swift CI pipeline for: ${goal}. Configure SwiftLint or swift-format: force_unwrapping/force_cast/force_try as errors. CI MUST use swift build and swift test only. Fail if .xcodeproj detected. CI matrix: macOS-latest + Linux (swift:latest Docker). swift test --enable-code-coverage with threshold. swift build -strict-concurrency=complete (fail on warnings). -warnings-as-errors set. SWIFT_DETERMINISTIC_HASHING=1 for reproducible ordering. Address sanitizer on nightly. Cache .build/ directory. Nightly job for Swift nightly toolchain. No '|| true' on any step.
verifies: force operations are errors not warnings
CI fails on Xcode project files

phases.checkpoint: CI pipeline enforces all Swift standards, zero tolerance
phases.failure_message: CI gate failed. Pipeline gaps or unenforced standards detected.

phases.name: Documentation
phases.principle: Is every public API documented with runnable examples?

delegates.task_template: Audit and perfect all Swift documentation for: ${goal}. Every public symbol MUST have /// doc comments compatible with DocC: single-line summary, Discussion for complex APIs, - Parameter name:, - Returns:, - Throws:, code examples in fenced blocks. Configure Swift-DocC plugin for CLI generation (not Xcode-hosted). Verify: swift package generate-documentation with zero warnings, static site export works, all public symbols in generated docs. Documentation.docc/ per target with landing page. CI builds docs with --warnings-as-errors. README with SPM installation and quickstart.
verifies: every public symbol has /// doc comment
DocC generates with zero warnings via CLI

delegates.task_template: Audit all Swift documentation and user-facing content for brand voice compliance for: ${goal}. Enforce the Euxis Standard: authoritative, concise, imperative mood. Cut all hedging ('you might', 'it seems'), filler ('basically', 'simply', 'just'), passive voice, and corporate jargon. Verify Apple documentation style: short declarative sentences, benefit before mechanism, one idea per sentence. Audit /// doc comments, README, DocC articles, and error messages. Output word count reduction and principle violations found.
verifies: brand voice audit complete with metrics

phases.checkpoint: All public APIs documented, DocC generates cleanly via CLI, brand voice enforced
phases.failure_message: Documentation gate failed. Missing docs, DocC warnings, or voice violations detected.

phases.name: API Design & UX
phases.principle: Is the API ergonomic, discoverable, and user-friendly?

delegates.task_template: Audit Swift API ergonomics for: ${goal}. Verify Swift API Design Guidelines: clarity at point of use, fluent English usage, factory methods use make prefix, boolean properties read as assertions (isEmpty). Verify error handling: custom Error types with LocalizedError (errorDescription), cases named by what went wrong. If CLI: ArgumentParser used, --help comprehensive, exit codes follow convention, NO_COLOR respected. If library: minimal generic constraints, default parameters reduce boilerplate, result builders where DSL improves readability. Verify CustomStringConvertible for user-facing types, CustomDebugStringConvertible for all public types.

phases.checkpoint: API follows Swift Design Guidelines, errors actionable, UX polished
phases.failure_message: API design gate failed. Design guideline violations or poor error messages detected.

phases.name: Hardening & Release
phases.principle: Is it release-ready with baselines, changelogs, and semver?

delegates.task_template: Stress test and establish performance baselines for: ${goal}. Benchmark hot paths with XCTest.measure or swift-benchmark. Set performance budgets: p50/p95/p99 latencies. Test with large inputs: 10x and 100x typical workload. Profile memory under sustained load: no unbounded growth, no leaked references, zero retain cycles under stress. Verify build time budget (debug <45s, release <180s). Check binary size: strip debug symbols, verify #if DEBUG isolation. Target: low memory, low CPU under sustained load.
verifies: no unbounded memory growth under load
low memory and CPU under sustained load

delegates.task_template: Perform exhaustive regression analysis for: ${goal}. Verify CI catches: performance regressions (XCTest baselines), coverage drops, new SwiftLint violations, new compiler warnings on nightly Swift toolchain. Classify every change by risk: actor isolation violations, Sendable conformance gaps, force-unwrap on optionals, retain cycles in closures, concurrency boundary shifts. Verify -strict-concurrency=complete regressions blocked. Scan for deprecated APIs older than 6 months. Verify dead code unreachable by any test. Check dev-only deps not leaked to production targets. Verify CI blocks regression merges.

Verify release readiness for: ${goal}. Execute pre-release checklist: all tests pass on macOS AND Linux, coverage at 100% on Core targets, SwiftLint clean, -strict-concurrency=complete clean, sanitizers pass, documentation current. Determine correct semantic version bump (MAJOR for breaking, MINOR for features, PATCH for fixes). Verify version consistent in Package.swift. Generate changelog from commit history following Keep a Changelog format. Verify upgrade/migration instructions for breaking changes. Draft release notes with highlights. Verify rollback plan exists. Coordinate security clearance with sentinel.

phases.checkpoint: Stress tested, regressions blocked, release-ready with semver and changelog
phases.failure_message: Hardening findings detected. Review stress tests, regressions, and release readiness.

phases.name: Final Truth Gate
phases.principle: Is every claim backed by evidence?

delegates.task_template: Final gate for: ${goal}. Validate evidence from all prior phases. Verify: no .xcodeproj files (SPM-pure), modular targets with matching Sources/Tests, async/await migration complete, -strict-concurrency=complete passes, zero retain cycles, all force operations justified or eliminated, best practices researched with citations, cross-platform parity macOS + Linux, 100% coverage on Core targets, SwiftLint zero violations, warnings-as-errors set, deps version-pinned and maintained with S0-S3 classification, DocC generates via CLI with zero warnings, brand voice audit passed, every public symbol documented, stress tests passed, regressions blocked, release readiness confirmed. Cross-check agent findings for contradictions. Issue PASS or FAIL.
verifies: all gate claims backed by evidence

delegates.task_template: Compliance audit for: ${goal}. Scan for PII exposure in logs, error messages, and debug traces. Audit all dependency licenses in Package.swift: reject copyleft (GPL, AGPL) in permissive projects without explicit approval. Verify copyright headers are present and correct across all Swift source files. Verify no secrets or credentials in source. Verify .gitignore excludes sensitive files. Check for unauthorized data flows to third-party endpoints. Flag any compliance violation with regulatory reference and remediation. Issue PASS, FAIL, or WARN verdict.
verifies: no PII in logs or error output
copyright headers present on all source files
no secrets or credentials in source

delegates.task_template: Adversarial review of all gate findings for: ${goal}. Challenge assumptions from every prior phase. For each gate's PASS claim: invert the assumption and test what happens if wrong. Challenge concurrency claims: does -strict-concurrency=complete truly guarantee safety, or are there @unchecked Sendable escapes? Challenge retain cycle claims: are all closure capture lists verified under all calling contexts? Perform pre-mortem: assume the package caused a production incident 6 months later, enumerate top 5 reasons. Assess blast radius. Evaluate reversibility. If all agents agree on PASS, verify it is not groupthink. Issue PROCEED, PROCEED WITH SAFEGUARDS, or RECONSIDER verdict.
verifies: retain cycle claims challenged under all contexts
blast radius assessed for remaining risks

phases.checkpoint: Reviewer sign-off, compliance cleared, critic challenge passed
phases.failure_message: Final truth gate failed. Unverified claims, compliance issues, or critic objections detected.
